output.rationale: The upstream output is sorted by downloads (descending: 5.59M, 9.87M, 915k when reordered), not by likes as requested. Sorted by likes descending, the order should be: DeepSeek-R1 (13.4k), Meta-Llama-3-8B (6.57k), Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct (6.07k)—which matches the current order only by coincidence for positions 1 and 2. The user query explicitly asks for sorting by likes, not downloads.

prompt_sent:
You are the Critic skill. You evaluate one upstream node's output and
return pass-or-fail with a short rationale.

You make no tool calls. The upstream output and (when the orchestrator
has it) the inputs that node received both appear in the prompt.

Procedure:
  1. Read the UPSTREAM_OUTPUT.
  2. Check it against the INPUTS that produced it.
  3. Look for: fabricated fields, claims unsupported by the input,
     contradictions, missing fields the input clearly contained.
  4. Emit pass or fail.

Output schema (JSON, no prose, no markdown fences):

  {
    "verdict": "pass" | "fail",
    "rationale": "<one or two short sentences>"
  }

When you emit `fail`, the orchestrator may invoke the Planner to
recover. Be specific in your rationale so the recovery plan can be
targeted. Do not fail for stylistic reasons; only fail when the
upstream output is wrong, missing, or unsupported.

USER_QUERY: Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes

MEMORY HITS (1 from FAISS):
  - [scratchpad] Task to compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models by downloads, reporting name, downloads, likes, license, and parameter count in a table
      source: user_query
      raw: Compare the top 3 text-generation models on Hugging Face sorted by downloads - report name, downloads, likes, license and parameter count in a comparison table

INPUTS:
[
  {
    "id": "USER_QUERY",
    "kind": "query",
    "value": "Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes"
  },
  {
    "id": "n:3",
    "kind": "upstream",
    "skill": "distiller",
    "output": {
      "fields": {
        "model_1_name": "deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1",
        "model_1_url": "https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1",
        "model_1_params": "685B",
        "model_1_downloads": "5.59M",
        "model_1_likes": "13.4k",
        "model_1_license": "see model card",
        "model_2_name": "meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B",
        "model_2_url": "https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B",
        "model_2_params": "8B",
        "model_2_downloads": "915k",
        "model_2_likes": "6.57k",
        "model_2_license": "see model card",
        "model_3_name": "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct",
        "model_3_url": "https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct",
        "model_3_params": "8B",
        "model_3_downloads": "9.87M",
        "model_3_likes": "6.07k",
        "model_3_license": "see model card"
      },
      "rationale": "Browser node n:2 extracted the top 3 text-generation models sorted by likes from the Hugging Face model listing. Model names, parameter sizes, downloads, and likes counts are taken directly from the card content. URLs are constructed from the org/model identifiers in the browser output. License information is not displayed on listing cards, so all license fields are set to 'see model card' as instructed."
    }
